tax: Terrae Elementalis Nobilis is placed at species rank beneath Terrae (Lithic order, Earth Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a precisely articulated layered-metamorphic body with a large-domed cranial mass, true stone fingers rather than mitten-hands, and a formation requirement of deep fold-pressure. It is separated from its sibling Terrae elementalis major by its precisely articulated layered-metamorphic anatomy with stone fingers and a large domed cranium versus the major form's coarse undifferentiated dark mass and mitten-hands; the two share the genus body-plan but never the the coarse undifferentiated major mass. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: layered gneiss, marble, and primary feldspar arranged in a differentiated humanoid body by deep fold-pressure; the stone types are qualitatively different from all other Terrae, not merely larger.
ap: Terrae Elementalis Nobilis stands 3–4 m and 10–20 tonnes — smaller than the major form but far more precisely structured. Its silhouette is a humanoid body with distinct layered banding across its surface, a clearly domed cranial mass larger relative to body than any other Terrae form, and individual stone digits on each hand rather than a mitten. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: distinct layered banding of gneiss and marble across the body surface, unlike the undifferentiated mixed stone of standard and major forms; individual articulated stone digits — five per hand — the only Terrae form with this trait; these leave five-point pressure marks rather than flat-edged impact marks; and a cranial mass that is one-quarter body width at its widest, visibly larger relative to body than in the standard or major form, and that carries a bright amber orbital glow visible at 150 m. It moves with a precisely balanced stride at 30 m tremor detection range — less than the major form's 100 m, because the layered body is better jointed and transfers less energy through the floor, and shows the brightest orbital glow of all Terrae forms, visible at 150 m in total darkness. Even after dispersal it can be identified from five-point hand-impressions 40 cm across where it has steadied itself, and layered gneiss-flakes shed along its path, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Terrae Elementalis Nobilis persists in deep fold-pressure zones where metamorphic rock has been repeatedly recrystallised — gneiss terrains, marble formations, and feldspar-rich deep intrusions; not confirmed in the active Gorge but inferred from the layered stone it sheds. It does not breed; it forms where deep fold-pressure acting on already-metamorphic rock recrystallises it into a precisely layered body, a process requiring more sustained and deeper pressure than the standard or major form, and recurs whenever the fold-pressure re-charges and the layered body re-forms, though the process takes far longer than standard fault-pressure reformation. It sustains its mass on deep fold-pressure acting on metamorphic stone, drawing in metamorphic rock incorporated at the fold — specifically the layered types that match its body-plan chemistry. Range is bounded by the deep metamorphic fold-pressure zone: outside that band the body sheds layered flakes progressively and loses digit articulation before the body itself fails, unlike the rubble-collapse of other forms. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a metamorphic fold-exposure where gneiss layers have begun moving in parallel rather than slipping against each other, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Terrae Elementalis Nobilis is roused by disturbance of the deep fold it stands in; seismic events rather than drilling, given the depth and otherwise lies a stratified standing mass that geologists initially classify as a metamorphic column. It claims a deep fold zone and the approach tunnels cut toward it and answers intrusion with communication attempts before force — the nobilis form is the only Terrae consistently reported to acknowledge grey-folk as distinct from random vibration, gesturing them away before striking. Toward grey-folk it is context-dependent; reported to gesture away non-threatening crews rather than strike immediately, though this is not universal and should not be relied upon; survey crews report that the Stonesong Heralds hold a formal protocol for approaching a nobilis site — three slow raps of a stone rod on the floor, spaced five seconds apart — which is understood to mean 'we are not drilling'. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Terrae Elementalis Nobilis: a body of layered gneiss, marble, and primary feldspar arranged in a differentiated humanoid body by deep fold-pressure; the stone types are qualitatively different from all other Terrae, not merely larger; immune to crushing, fire, and vibration more effectively than other Terrae forms; the layered gneiss body absorbs and redirects vibration rather than resonating with it; and a defining weakness — targeted acid on the marble layers, which dissolves the binding chemistry; this fails the body more rapidly than any vibration approach. Its senses run through the medium itself: tremor sensing at 300 m through fold-pressure; pressure sensing that extends to adjacent fault systems not directly connected; the large cranial mass is associated with an expanded sensing range in all Stonesong Herald records. It tolerates extreme heat, deep pressure, and total darkness but fails at targeted acid on the marble binding layers. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the layered banding, domed cranial mass, individual stone digits, and 150 m orbital glow, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Terrae Elementalis Nobilis are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it can redirect vibration through its layered body rather than resonating with it, making standard Terrae dispersal approaches ineffective; it shapes stone with its articulated digits rather than mass-striking — it can notch, groove, and seal passages with precision; it can communicate intent through the floor to other Terrae in its fold zone, alerting standard and major forms to a threat; and it repairs from its specific metamorphic stone types selectively — it will not incorporate basalt or sandstone, unlike other Terrae. Each is a direct expression of deep fold-pressure acting on a precisely layered metamorphic body with an enlarged cranial sensing mass, so it cannot, for instance, be found in shallow fault zones, move quickly, or form in the active Gorge at recorded pressure depths. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Terrae Elementalis Nobilis matters because its five-point hand-impressions and gneiss-flake trail, if found in the Gorge's deeper surveys, would indicate a fold-pressure zone too deep and metamorphic for the current working tiers — a significant finding for Forgemaster route-planning. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as not an active field hazard in the Gorge; retained as a context entry and a comparison form for Terrae anatomy classification, and its presence reshapes routes through no active route impact; five-point hand-impressions in a deep survey passage would close the lease pending Herald assessment. Stonesong Herald oral records refer to the nobilis form as the 'deep counsel' — not a ruler, but a body that the stone makes when it has the time and pressure to be precise. The three-rap protocol is old enough that no Herald alive knows who taught it.
g: Terrae Elementalis Nobilis (Terrae elementalis nobilis) is the high-form earth elemental: a rare Terrae body-plan distinguished from the standard, minor, and major forms by a larger and more complex cranial mass, articulated stone digits rather than mitten-hands, and formation only at exceptional deep-fold pressure — a heritable anatomy, not a rank. Documented from the deepest tectonic fold-pressure zones; not confirmed in the active Gorge, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a precisely articulated stone humanoid of layered gneiss, marble, and primary feldspar, held by deep fold-pressure — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. The nobilis form has been treated as a 'prince' class in many cultural traditions, but this journal classifies it by anatomy: it is a distinct body-plan with a larger cranial mass, articulated stone digits, and layered metamorphic stone that no other Terrae form shares. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a carefully stratified collapse of layered gneiss and marble that preserves rough limb outlines longer than other forms and no carcass.
